games.name: Yahtzee
games.tagline: Five dice, thirteen shots at glory
games.equipment: 5 standard dice, scorecard per player, pencil
games.objective: Score points by rolling five dice to make specific combinations across 13 categories. The player with the highest total score wins.

setup.title: Gather equipment
setup.description: Each player needs a scorecard. You need 5 standard six-sided dice and a cup for rolling (optional but traditional).

setup.title: Determine turn order
setup.description: Each player rolls one die. The highest roller goes first. In case of a tie, those players re-roll.

setup.title: Understand the scorecard
setup.description: The scorecard has 13 scoring categories: 6 in the Upper Section (Aces through Sixes) and 7 in the Lower Section (Three of a Kind, Four of a Kind, Full House, Small Straight, Large Straight, Yahtzee, Chance).

gameplay.title: Roll all five dice
gameplay.description: On your turn, roll all five dice. This is your first roll. You must roll at least once per turn.

gameplay.title: Decide which to keep
gameplay.description: After the first roll, set aside any dice you want to keep. You can keep as many or as few as you like—even none.

gameplay.title: Roll remaining dice (2nd roll)
gameplay.description: Re-roll any dice you didn't keep. You now have up to one more re-roll remaining.

gameplay.title: Final re-roll (optional)
gameplay.description: Optionally re-roll any dice one last time for your third and final roll. You cannot roll more than 3 times total per turn.

gameplay.title: Score your dice
gameplay.description: After your final roll, you MUST mark a score in exactly one unfilled category on your scorecard. If no category fits, you must mark a zero in any unused category.

gameplay.title: Pass to next player
gameplay.description: Play passes clockwise. Each player takes turns until all 13 categories are filled. Then final scores are tallied.

scoring.name: Aces
scoring.description: Count and add only the dice showing 1

scoring.name: Twos
scoring.description: Count and add only the dice showing 2

scoring.name: Threes
scoring.description: Count and add only the dice showing 3

scoring.name: Fours
scoring.description: Count and add only the dice showing 4

scoring.name: Fives
scoring.description: Count and add only the dice showing 5

scoring.name: Sixes
scoring.description: Count and add only the dice showing 6

scoring.name: Upper Section Bonus
scoring.description: If your total for the Upper Section is 63 or more, you earn a bonus
scoring.example: Score 63+ in Aces–Sixes to earn 35 extra points

scoring.name: Three of a Kind
scoring.description: At least three dice showing the same number

scoring.name: Four of a Kind
scoring.description: At least four dice showing the same number

scoring.name: Full House
scoring.description: Three of one number AND two of another number

scoring.name: Yahtzee
scoring.description: All five dice showing the same number
scoring.points: 50 points (first); 100-point bonus for each additional Yahtzee

scoring.name: Chance
scoring.example: Roll: 1,2,3,4,5 → Score: 15. Use this as a dump for bad rolls.

variations.name: Double Yahtzee Bonus
variations.description: Some house rules award 200 points (instead of 100) for each Yahtzee after the first.

variations.name: Forced Joker Rule
variations.description: If you roll a Yahtzee but already scored 50 in the Yahtzee box, you can use it as a joker in the Upper or Lower Section.

variations.name: Triple Yahtzee
variations.description: Play three rounds simultaneously using three separate scorecards. Scores from column 2 are doubled, column 3 are tripled.

variations.name: Painted Dice
variations.description: Replace standard dice with colored dice — each color has unique rules. A fun variant for experienced players.

sampleTurn.description: Aiming for Yahtzee (five of a kind). Player starts with a lucky opening roll.

rolls.note: Three 5s already! Keep them all, re-roll the 3 and 2.
Four 5s! Keep all four, re-roll the 1 for one last shot.
YAHTZEE! All five dice show 5. Score 50 points!

games.name: Texas Hold'em Poker
games.tagline: Two cards. Your poker face. The rest is luck (and nerve).
games.objective: Win chips by making the best 5-card poker hand using any combination of your 2 hole cards and 5 community cards, or by forcing all opponents to fold.

setup.title: Set up the table
setup.description: Use a standard 52-card deck. Each player starts with an equal number of chips. Assign a dealer button.

setup.title: Post the blinds
setup.description: The player to the left of the dealer posts the Small Blind. The player to their left posts the Big Blind. Blinds start the pot.

setup.title: Deal hole cards
setup.description: The dealer deals 2 cards face-down to each player. These private cards are your 'hole cards'—never show them to opponents.

gameplay.title: Pre-Flop betting
gameplay.description: Starting with the player left of the big blind, each player can: CALL (match the big blind), RAISE (increase the bet), or FOLD (discard cards).

gameplay.title: The Flop
gameplay.description: The dealer burns one card then deals 3 community cards face-up. These are shared by all players. A new betting round begins.

gameplay.title: The Turn
gameplay.description: The dealer burns another card and deals 1 more community card face-up (the 4th). Another betting round follows.

gameplay.title: The River
gameplay.description: The dealer burns a final card and deals the 5th and last community card. The final betting round takes place.

gameplay.title: The Showdown
gameplay.description: If two or more players remain, they reveal their hole cards. Best 5-card hand using any combination of hole cards and community cards wins the pot.

gameplay.title: Next hand
gameplay.description: The dealer button moves one position clockwise. New blinds are posted and a new hand begins.

handRankings.name: Royal Flush
handRankings.description: The highest possible hand — five highest cards of the same suit

handRankings.name: Straight Flush
handRankings.description: Five consecutive cards of the same suit

handRankings.name: Four of a Kind
handRankings.description: Four cards of the same rank

handRankings.name: Full House
handRankings.description: Three of a kind plus a pair

handRankings.name: Flush
handRankings.description: Any five cards of the same suit (not consecutive)

handRankings.name: Straight
handRankings.description: Five consecutive cards of any suits

handRankings.name: Three of a Kind
handRankings.description: Three cards of the same rank

handRankings.name: One Pair
handRankings.description: Two cards of the same rank

bettingRounds.name: Pre-Flop
bettingRounds.description: First round. Action starts left of the big blind. Players must at least call the big blind to stay in.

bettingRounds.name: Flop
bettingRounds.description: Second round. First 3 shared cards are dealt. Action starts from the first active player left of the dealer.

bettingRounds.name: Turn
bettingRounds.description: Third round. One more shared card. Bets often double in limit games.

bettingRounds.name: River
bettingRounds.description: Final round. Last shared card. Last chance to bet, bluff, or extract value before showdown.

variations.name: No-Limit Texas Hold'em
variations.description: Players can bet any amount up to all their chips at any time ('all-in'). Used in the World Series of Poker Main Event.

variations.name: Pot-Limit Hold'em
variations.description: Players can only bet up to the current size of the pot. Builds more slowly than No-Limit.

variations.name: Limit Hold'em
variations.description: Bets and raises are fixed amounts. Pre-flop and flop bets equal the big blind; turn and river bets are doubled.

variations.name: Short Deck Hold'em
variations.description: Played with a 36-card deck (2s-5s removed). Hand rankings shift — a flush beats a full house.

sampleHand.description: A classic strong hand scenario showing how community cards change the game.

action.description: You're dealt A♠ K♠ (Big Slick). You raise 3x the big blind. One opponent calls.
You flop three Kings! You bet half the pot to extract value. Opponent calls.
The 2♦ doesn't help your opponent. You bet again. Opponent raises! You call.
The Ace gives you a Full House: Kings full of Aces. You move all-in. Opponent calls with K♣ 7♦ — your Aces beat their Sevens!

sampleHand.result: You win with Kings full of Aces (K♠ K♦ K♥ A♠ A♦)

games.name: Ride the Bus
games.tagline: The only game where losing is part of the fun
games.ageWarning: This is a drinking game — play responsibly, 21+ only.
games.objective: Avoid being the player with the most wrong guesses across 4 rounds, or you'll 'ride the bus' — a 10-card gauntlet where you must guess red or black in sequence without a single mistake.

setup.title: Prepare the deck
setup.description: Remove jokers from a standard 52-card deck and shuffle thoroughly. Aces are HIGH throughout the game.

setup.title: Choose a dealer
setup.description: Designate one player as the dealer. The dealer flips and manages all cards but does not participate in guessing rounds.

setup.title: Sit in a circle
setup.description: All players sit in a circle with their drinks ready. Each player collects one card per round, building a 4-card hand by the end.

setup.title: Understand the flow
setup.description: The game has 4 guessing rounds, followed by a Pyramid phase, followed by a 'Ride the Bus' penalty for the most wrong guesses.

gameplay.title: Round 1 — Red or Black?
gameplay.description: Each player guesses red (♥♦) or black (♠♣). The dealer flips the top card for each player. Wrong = 1 drink.

gameplay.title: Round 2 — Higher or Lower?
gameplay.description: Using Round 1 card as reference, guess if the next card is higher or lower. Ace is highest. Wrong = 2 drinks.

gameplay.title: Round 3 — Inside or Outside?
gameplay.description: Guess if the next card falls between or outside the range of your two existing cards. Wrong = 3 drinks.

gameplay.title: Round 4 — Guess the Suit
gameplay.description: Guess which suit (♠ ♥ ♦ ♣) your next card will be. Pure 25% chance. Wrong = 4 drinks.

gameplay.title: The Pyramid
gameplay.description: Dealer builds a 5-4-3-2-1 pyramid of face-down cards. Any player holding a matching rank can assign drinks to anyone else.

gameplay.title: Ride the Bus
gameplay.description: The player with the most wrong guesses must ride the bus: guess red or black for 10 cards in a row. Wrong = drink and restart from card 1. Get all 10 right to escape!

scoring: Track wrong guesses per player across all 4 rounds
The player with the most wrong guesses rides the bus
If two or more players tie, all tied players ride the bus — taking turns guessing
There is no traditional winner — the game is about fun and avoiding the bus!
strategyTips: Round 2: If your card is very high (Q, K, A) guess Lower; if very low (2, 3) guess Higher.
Round 3: The wider the gap between your two cards, the better your 'Inside' odds are.
Round 4: Pure 25% luck. Some players always pick their favorite suit and commit.
Pyramid: Pay attention to what ranks other players hold so you know who can dish out drinks.
Bus phase: There's no strategy — just stay calm and hope the cards cooperate!

variations.name: Irish Poker
variations.description: Skip the pyramid and bus phases entirely. Just play the 4 guessing rounds as a simpler standalone drinking game.

variations.name: Mercy Rule
variations.description: If the bus rider fails to complete the bus after 5 full restart attempts, the group may show mercy and end the phase.

variations.name: Team Ride
variations.description: Players pair up into teams of 2. If one teammate rides the bus, their partner joins and they alternate guesses.

variations.name: Double or Nothing
variations.description: During the bus, the rider can call 'double or nothing' before a guess. Correct = skip ahead 2 cards. Wrong = go back 2 cards.

variations.name: No Pyramid
variations.description: Skip the pyramid phase entirely and determine who rides the bus solely based on wrong guess counts from Rounds 1–4.

sampleHand.description: One player's unlucky journey through all 4 rounds — straight to the bus.

action.description: Guessed Black → flipped 7♣. Correct! No drinks. Starting strong.
Had 7♣. Guessed Higher → flipped 3♥. Wrong! Drink 2.
Had 7♣ and 3♥. Guessed Inside → flipped J♠. Outside the range! Drink 3.
Guessed ♥ → flipped K♦. Wrong suit. Drink 4. A brutal round.

sampleHand.result: Total: 9 drinks, 3 wrong guesses — you're definitely riding the bus tonight!

games.name: Teen Patti
games.tagline: India's poker — where bluffing is an art form
games.equipment: Standard 52-card deck, chips or coins for betting
games.objective: Win the pot by having the best 3-card hand or by forcing all other players to fold.

setup.title: Set the boot (ante)
setup.description: Agree on a minimum and maximum bet (the boot amount). Everyone must put in the boot to create the initial pot.

setup.title: Place the boot
setup.description: Each player puts in the boot (ante) to create the initial pot before any cards are dealt.

setup.title: Choose the dealer
setup.description: Designate a dealer — this role rotates clockwise each hand.

setup.title: Prepare the deck
setup.description: Remove jokers and shuffle thoroughly. Aces are HIGH in standard Teen Patti.

setup.title: Deal three cards
setup.description: Dealer deals 3 cards face-down to each player, one at a time, going clockwise.

gameplay.description: Each player decides whether to play 'seen' (look at your cards) or 'blind' (don't look). Blind players bet half the amount of seen players and can switch to seen at any time.
gameplay.note: Playing blind gives you a betting cost advantage — you pay half — but you're flying blind on your hand.

gameplay.description: Starting left of the dealer, each player can: CALL (match current bet), RAISE (increase bet), or FOLD (pack/quit). Betting continues clockwise until all active players match the highest bet or fold.
rules: Seen players must bet at least the current stake (chaal)
Blind players bet half the current stake
Maximum raise is typically 2x the current stake (varies by house rules)
Once seen, you cannot go back to blind

gameplay.description: A seen player can request a private comparison (sideshow) with the previous player, if that player is also seen. The weaker hand must fold. The requested player can accept or deny.
gameplay.note: Sideshow costs the current stake and is a strategic tool to eliminate players without going to full showdown.

gameplay.description: When only 2 players remain, either can force a show by paying double the current stake. The best 3-card hand wins the entire pot. If only 1 player remains (all others folded), they win without showing cards.
gameplay.note: If a blind player and seen player remain, the blind player can ask for show at the current stake; the seen player pays double.

handRankings.name: Trail / Trio (Three of a Kind)
handRankings.description: Three cards of the same rank
handRankings.note: A-A-A is highest, 2-2-2 is lowest trail

handRankings.name: Pure Sequence (Straight Flush)
handRankings.description: Three consecutive cards of the same suit
handRankings.note: A-K-Q is highest; A-2-3 is second highest; 4-3-2 is lowest

handRankings.name: Sequence / Run (Straight)
handRankings.description: Three consecutive cards of different suits
handRankings.note: A-K-Q is highest; A-2-3 is second highest (wraparound)

handRankings.name: Color (Flush)
handRankings.description: Three cards of the same suit, not in sequence
handRankings.note: Compare highest card, then second, then third. A-K-J of a suit is the best flush.

handRankings.name: Pair
handRankings.description: Two cards of the same rank
handRankings.note: A-A-X is the highest pair; 2-2-X is the lowest

handRankings.name: High Card
handRankings.note: Compare highest card, then second, then third. A-K-J is the best high card hand.

betting: Boot: Initial ante all players pay before the deal
Chaal: The current bet amount players must match
Blind bet: Half the current chaal (if playing blind)
Seen bet: Full current chaal (if playing seen)
Raise: Typically limited to 2x the current chaal
Show: Costs 2x the current chaal (if you force a comparison)
rules: Aces are HIGH in standard Teen Patti
A-2-3 is a valid sequence (wraparound) and is the second-best sequence after A-K-Q
Blind players can look at their cards at any time and become seen players
Once seen, you cannot go back to blind
Sideshow can only be requested after a minimum number of betting rounds (house rules)
The player requesting a sideshow pays the current stake; the requested player can accept or deny
Pack / Fold: Forfeiting cards and exiting the hand; your boot contribution stays in the pot
The player who wins without showing cards does not have to reveal their hand
strategyTips: Playing blind gives you cost advantage — consider staying blind with medium hands to save chips
Aggressive betting as a blind player can force folds even if you're bluffing
Position matters — acting last gives you information advantage over early bettors
Use sideshow strategically to eliminate players cheaply when you're confident in your hand
Trail (three of a kind) is rare — if you have it, bet heavily and don't tip your hand too early
Pure sequence beats regular sequence — don't confuse them when evaluating your hand
Watch betting patterns — sudden aggression after staying calm often signals a strong hand
Mix up your seen/blind play style so opponents can't read you
culturalNotes: Teen Patti means 'three cards' in Hindi
Extremely popular during Diwali celebrations across India
Considered a social game — often played for hours at family gatherings
Many regional variations exist across different states of India
Traditionally played with real stakes, but can be played with chips or points
Similar to the British game 'Three Card Brag' and some poker variants

variations.name: Muflis (Lowball)
variations.description: Hand rankings are reversed — worst hand wins. High card beats a trail. Great for mixing things up.

variations.name: AK47
variations.description: A, K, 4, and 7 of any suit are wild/joker cards and can complete any hand.

variations.name: Best of Four
variations.description: Each player gets 4 cards and makes the best 3-card hand from them, adding one decision point.

variations.name: Community Cards
variations.description: 3 community cards dealt face-up that all players combine with their hole cards, similar to Texas Hold'em.

variations.name: Sudden Death
variations.description: No sideshows allowed. Betting continues until only one player remains or a showdown is forced.

variations.name: Rotating Jokers
variations.description: After each round, a different rank becomes wild (first round 2s are wild, then 3s, and so on).

sampleHand.description: Example hand with 4 players — a tense round that ends in a showdown.

players.name: Player 1
players.action: Played seen, bet 20, stayed in to showdown

players.name: Player 3
players.action: Played seen, folded after Player 1 raised

sampleHand.outcome: Player 1 forces show against Player 2. Player 2 reveals 8♠ 5♥ 2♦ (high card). Player 1's pair of Kings wins the pot!

games.name: Blackjack
games.tagline: Beat the dealer to 21 — math optional, nerve required
games.objective: Beat the dealer by getting a hand value closer to 21 without going over (busting). Ties push (your bet is returned).
cardValues: Face cards (J, Q, K): Worth 10 each
Ace: Worth 1 or 11 — whichever is more favorable
Soft hand: Contains an Ace counted as 11 (e.g., A-6 = soft 17)
Hard hand: No Ace, or Ace counted as 1 (e.g., 10-7 = hard 17)

setup.title: Designate a dealer
setup.description: One player is the dealer (in a casino, this is the house). The dealer follows fixed rules — no player decisions.

setup.title: Shuffle the decks
setup.description: Shuffle 1 or more decks together. Casinos typically use 6–8 decks in a shoe to prevent card counting.

setup.title: Place your bet
setup.description: Each player places their bet in the designated betting circle before any cards are dealt.

setup.title: Deal initial cards
setup.description: Dealer gives each player 2 cards face-up, then deals themselves 2 cards (1 face-up, 1 face-down/hole card).

setup.title: Check for Blackjack
setup.description: If any player or the dealer has Blackjack (Ace + 10-value card) on the first 2 cards, it's announced immediately.

gameplay.description: If dealer's up card is an Ace or 10-value card, dealer checks the hole card for Blackjack. If dealer has Blackjack, all player bets lose (unless player also has Blackjack — that's a push).
gameplay.note: Player Blackjack typically pays 3:2 (bet $10, win $15). Avoid tables that pay 6:5!

gameplay.description: Starting from dealer's left, each player decides their action. The goal is to get as close to 21 as possible without busting.

actions.name: HIT
actions.description: Take another card. Can hit multiple times until you're satisfied or bust (exceed 21).

actions.name: STAND
actions.description: Keep your current hand and take no more cards. Pass to the next player.

actions.name: DOUBLE DOWN
actions.description: Double your bet and receive exactly one more card, then you must stand. Usually only allowed on your first 2 cards.

actions.name: SPLIT
actions.description: If your first 2 cards are the same value, split into 2 separate hands with equal bets on each. Play each independently.

actions.name: SURRENDER
actions.description: Forfeit half your bet and end the hand immediately. Not available in all games — check table rules first.

gameplay.description: After all players finish, the dealer reveals their hole card and must follow fixed rules — no choices allowed.
rules: Dealer must HIT on 16 or less
Dealer must STAND on 17 or more
Some casinos require dealer to hit soft 17 (A-6) — check table rules
Dealer has no choices — always plays by the house rules

gameplay.description: Compare each player's hand to the dealer's hand to determine winners.
outcomes: Player busts (over 21): Lose bet immediately, regardless of dealer's hand
Dealer busts: All remaining players win even money (1:1)
Player total higher than dealer: Win even money
Dealer total higher: Player loses bet
Same total: Push (tie) — bet is returned
Player Blackjack vs dealer non-Blackjack: Win 3:2

basicStrategy.situation: Hard Totals (no Ace, or Ace counted as 1)
rules: 17 or higher: Always STAND
13–16: STAND if dealer shows 2–6, HIT if dealer shows 7–Ace
12: STAND if dealer shows 4–6, HIT otherwise
11: DOUBLE if dealer shows 2–10, HIT vs Ace
10: DOUBLE if dealer shows 2–9, HIT vs 10 or Ace
9: DOUBLE if dealer shows 3–6, HIT otherwise
8 or less: Always HIT (can't bust)
Soft 19–20 (A-8, A-9): Always STAND
Soft 18 (A-7): STAND vs dealer 2, 7, 8; DOUBLE vs 3–6; HIT vs 9, 10, Ace
Soft 17 (A-6): DOUBLE vs dealer 3–6, HIT otherwise
Soft 16 or less: HIT (or DOUBLE vs dealer 4–6)
Always SPLIT: Aces and 8s
Never SPLIT: 10s (20 is a great hand), 5s (treat as 10 — Double instead), 4s
SPLIT 2s, 3s, 6s, 7s if dealer shows 2–7
SPLIT 9s if dealer shows 2–6 or 8–9 (stand against 7, 10, or Ace)
Dealer must follow fixed rules — hit on 16 or less, stand on 17 or more
Player Blackjack beats dealer 21 made with 3 or more cards
After splitting Aces, you usually only get one additional card per Ace
21 made after splitting Aces is not Blackjack and pays 1:1, not 3:2
Double down is usually only allowed on your initial 2 cards
Surrender forfeits half your bet and must be your first action (late surrender = after dealer checks for BJ)
Insurance is a side bet that dealer has Blackjack — it pays 2:1 but is generally a bad bet
Dealer Blackjack takes all player bets except player Blackjacks (those push)
strategyTips: Never take insurance (the house edge on insurance is over 7%)
Always split Aces and 8s — no exceptions
Never split 10s — standing on 20 is almost always the right move
Double down on 11 when dealer shows 2–10
Dealer is most likely to bust when showing 4, 5, or 6 (weak upcards)
Never hit on 17 or higher (hard total)
Memorize the basic strategy chart — it reduces the house edge to under 0.5%
House edge with perfect basic strategy is typically around 0.5%
Surrender 16 against dealer 9, 10, or Ace (if surrender is available)
Hit soft 17 or lower — you can't bust on the first hit since the Ace can drop to 1
casinoRules: Minimum and maximum bets are posted at each table
Most casinos use 6 or 8 decks in a shoe
Blackjack pays 3:2 at good tables — avoid tables that pay 6:5
Dealer stands on all 17s OR hits soft 17, depending on casino
A cut card signals when to reshuffle
Tipping the dealer is customary after a winning session

variations.name: European Blackjack
variations.description: Dealer receives only 1 card initially, gets hole card after all players finish. No early Blackjack check.

variations.name: Spanish 21
variations.description: All 10s removed from the deck (48-card deck). Player 21 always wins. Bonus payouts for special hands like 6-7-8 or 7-7-7.

variations.name: Double Exposure
variations.description: Both dealer cards are dealt face-up. Blackjacks pay even money. Dealer wins all ties except player Blackjack.

variations.name: Blackjack Switch
variations.description: Play 2 hands simultaneously and can switch the second card dealt to each hand. Blackjacks pay 1:1. Dealer 22 pushes all.

variations.name: Pontoon
variations.description: British version. Both dealer cards face-down. 'Pontoon' pays 2:1. 5-card trick (5 cards without busting) beats everything except Pontoon.

sampleHand.description: Three players, three different outcomes against the dealer.

players.name: Player 2
players.action: SPLIT 7s — first hand gets 10♠ (17), second hand hits 4♥ then 9♠ (20)
players.outcome: First hand (17) loses to dealer 20. Second hand (20) pushes. Net: lose 1 unit.

games.name: Craps
games.tagline: The most social game in the casino, two dice at a time
games.equipment: Two standard 6-sided dice, chips for betting, craps table (or any flat surface for Street Craps)
games.objective: Bet on the outcome of dice rolls. On the Pass Line: win if the shooter rolls 7 or 11 on the first roll, or hits their point number before rolling a 7.

setup.title: Choose the shooter
setup.description: Designate the first shooter (the player rolling the dice). The shooter rotates clockwise after each 'seven out'.

setup.title: Select the dice
setup.description: The shooter selects 2 dice from a set offered by the stickman (in casino) or from available dice.

setup.title: Place your bets
setup.description: All players place their bets before the shooter throws. Shooter must bet Pass Line or Don't Pass to roll.

setup.title: Start with Come Out roll
setup.description: When the puck is OFF, it's a Come Out roll — the start of a new round. This establishes whether a point is set.

setup.title: Track the point
setup.description: If a point is set (4, 5, 6, 8, 9, or 10), the dealer places the puck ON that number on the table layout.

gameplay.description: The first roll of a new round determines the outcome immediately or sets a point number.

outcomes.result: Pass Line bets WIN instantly. Don't Pass bets LOSE. New come out roll begins.
Pass Line bets LOSE instantly. Don't Pass bets WIN (except 12 is a push in most casinos). New come out roll.
This number becomes the POINT. Dealer marks it with the puck (flipped to ON). Enter Point Phase.

gameplay.description: After a point is established, the shooter keeps rolling until they hit the point number again (win) or roll a 7 (seven out, lose).

outcomes.result: Pass Line WIN. Don't Pass LOSE. Point is made — new come out roll with same shooter.
Pass Line LOSE. Don't Pass WIN. Shooter loses the dice. New shooter, new come out roll.
No effect on Pass/Don't Pass. Other bets (Place, Field, Come) may resolve. Shooter rolls again.

basicBets.name: Pass Line
basicBets.description: Bet that the shooter will win (make their point before rolling 7). Place before the come out roll.
basicBets.strategy: Best bet for beginners. You're cheering for the shooter with the table.

basicBets.name: Don't Pass
basicBets.description: Bet against the shooter — you win if they seven out. Note: 12 on come out is a push, not a win.
basicBets.strategy: Slightly better odds than Pass Line but socially awkward at a hot table (you're rooting against everyone).

basicBets.name: Come Bet
basicBets.description: Same as Pass Line but placed after a point is established. The next roll becomes your personal point.
basicBets.strategy: Great for continuous action and covering multiple numbers.

basicBets.name: Don't Come
basicBets.description: Opposite of Come bet. Placed after a point is established — you win if a 7 rolls before your come point.
basicBets.strategy: Combine with Don't Pass for a consistent 'dark side' strategy.

basicBets.name: Odds Bet
basicBets.description: An additional bet placed behind your Pass/Don't Pass after a point is set. This bet has ZERO house edge — it pays true mathematical odds.
basicBets.payout: 2:1 on point 4 or 10 | 3:2 on point 5 or 9 | 6:5 on point 6 or 8
basicBets.strategy: ALWAYS take maximum odds. This is the single best bet in any casino — the house has no edge on it.
basicBets.note: Casinos allow 1x, 2x, 3x, 5x, or even 100x odds depending on their rules.

advancedBets.name: Place Bets (6 or 8)
advancedBets.description: Bet that 6 or 8 will roll before a 7. Available any time, on any number (4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 10).
advancedBets.strategy: Place 6 and 8 are solid bets. Avoid Place 4/10 — the house edge jumps to 6.67%.

advancedBets.name: Field Bet
advancedBets.description: One-roll bet that the next roll is 2, 3, 4, 9, 10, 11, or 12 (7 out of 11 numbers).
advancedBets.payout: 1:1 on most numbers; 2:1 on 2; 2:1 or 3:1 on 12 (varies)
advancedBets.strategy: Popular with newer players but the 7 has six ways to roll — it's not as good as it looks.

advancedBets.name: Big 6 / Big 8
advancedBets.description: Bet that 6 or 8 will roll before a 7. Looks simple but pays only 1:1.
advancedBets.strategy: NEVER make this bet — Place bets on 6/8 pay 7:6 for the same outcome. Big 6/8 is a sucker bet.

advancedBets.name: Hardways (4, 6, 8, 10)
advancedBets.description: Bet that the number rolls as doubles (e.g., 3-3 for hard 6) before a 7 or an 'easy' combination.
advancedBets.strategy: Fun to watch but bad for your bankroll. Think of it as entertainment, not strategy.

advancedBets.name: Any 7
advancedBets.description: One-roll bet that the next roll is a 7. Simple, but terrible value.
advancedBets.strategy: The worst bet on the table. Avoid completely.

advancedBets.name: Any Craps
advancedBets.description: One-roll bet that the next roll is 2, 3, or 12.
advancedBets.strategy: Sometimes used as insurance on the come out roll but long-term it's a losing proposition.

advancedBets.name: Horn Bet
advancedBets.description: One-roll bet split equally on 2, 3, 11, and 12 simultaneously.
advancedBets.strategy: Flashy and exciting — and terrible for your wallet. Dealers love taking these bets for their tips.

diceRollProbabilities: 2 or 12: 1 way to roll (2.78%) — Snake eyes or Midnight
3 or 11: 2 ways to roll each (5.56%) — Ace-deuce or Yo-eleven
6 or 8: 5 ways to roll each (13.89%) — Most common non-7
7: 6 ways to roll (16.67%) — Most likely single outcome
rules: The shooter must have a Pass or Don't Pass bet to roll
Dice must hit the back wall of the table to be a valid roll
If dice leave the table, they're inspected before re-use (or replaced)
The shooter keeps rolling until they seven out (roll 7 after point is set)
Pass Line and Come bets cannot be removed once placed after a point is set
Don't Pass and Don't Come bets can be removed anytime (but you shouldn't — you have the edge)
Puck OFF = come out roll; Puck ON = point is established
Bets can be placed, changed, or removed between rolls (except Pass/Come after point)
strategyTips: Stick to Pass Line + maximum Odds bet for the best mathematical edge
Place 6 and 8 are acceptable if you want action on multiple numbers
Avoid all proposition bets in the middle of the table — they have terrible house edges
Never bet Any 7 — it's the worst bet in the casino at 16.67% house edge
Don't Pass + Odds is mathematically slightly better than Pass + Odds but will make you unpopular
Take the maximum odds allowed — it's the only true 0% house edge bet in the casino
Field bets look attractive (covering 7 numbers) but the 7 alone has more ways to roll than 2+12
Hardways and Horn bets are entertainment money — budget for them separately
Craps has some of the best odds in the casino if you stick to Pass/Don't Pass + Odds
Come bets are great for spreading action across multiple point numbers
etiquette: Never say 'seven' at the craps table — it's considered extremely bad luck
Keep hands out of the table when dice are rolling — stickman calls 'dice are out'
Tip the dealers by placing a bet for them on the Pass Line
Don't pass dice from hand to hand — use one hand when handling them
Have your bets ready between rolls — don't delay the game
Cheer for the shooter (unless you're on the Don't side)
Don't touch your chips once dice are rolling
Late bets after the dice are thrown are refused — bet between rolls
terminology: Shooter: The player currently rolling the dice
Come Out Roll: The first roll of a new round (puck is OFF)
Point: The number (4, 5, 6, 8, 9, or 10) that must be rolled again to win
Seven Out: Rolling a 7 after the point is established — shooter loses the dice
Natural: Rolling 7 or 11 on come out — automatic Pass Line win
Craps: Rolling 2, 3, or 12 on come out — automatic Pass Line loss
Stickman: Casino employee who controls the dice with a long stick
Boxman: Casino supervisor seated at the table
Puck: Disc marker showing whether a point is On or Off
Yo / Yo-leven: How dealers call 11 (to avoid confusion with 7)
Easy Way: Making an even number without doubles (e.g., 5-3 for 8)

variations.name: Street Craps
variations.description: Casual version without a table. Only Pass/Don't Pass bets. Shooter rolls against a wall. Popular in informal settings — just dice and betting.

variations.name: Crapless Craps
variations.description: 2, 3, 11, and 12 all become point numbers on come out roll — you can't 'crap out'. Higher house edge but no instant losses.

variations.name: High Point Craps
variations.description: 2 and 3 are ignored on come out. 11 and 12 win automatically. Point must be 4 or higher.

variations.name: New York Craps
variations.description: No Come/Don't Come bets. House takes a 5% commission on winning bets. Common on the East Coast.

variations.name: Die Rich Craps
variations.description: Single-die variation. Roll 6 to start, then try to roll 6 again before rolling 1. Simple but tense.

sampleRound.description: One player's Pass Line bet through a complete round.

rolls.result: Point is 6! Dealer flips puck to ON.
Rolled 8 — not the point. Keep rolling!
Rolled 3 — not the point. Keep rolling!

rolls.shooterStatus: Loses dice — next player becomes the new shooter
